name: Real-Time Event AI
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure Event Hubs
services.purpose: High-throughput event ingestion — millions of events/second from IoT sensors, application logs, transaction streams, clickstreams, and telemetry feeds

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: Event processing pipeline — real-time stream processing, feature extraction, anomaly detection dispatch, alert routing, and state machine management

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: AI anomaly analysis — contextual interpretation of detected anomalies, root cause hypothesis generation, severity classification, and natural-language alert summaries

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput for guaranteed sub-second analysis

services.name: Azure Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Event state and anomaly store — sliding window aggregations, anomaly history, detection rules, baseline metrics, and alert tracking with sub-10ms reads

services.name: Azure SignalR Service
services.purpose: Real-time push notifications — live dashboard updates, anomaly alerts to operators, event stream visualization, and WebSocket-based client communication

services.name: Azure Stream Analytics
services.purpose: Windowed aggregation engine — sliding/tumbling/hopping windows for baseline computation, statistical anomaly detection, pattern matching, and temporal joins

enterprise.note: 24 streaming units, multi-stream joins, reference data

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Event Hub connection strings, Azure OpenAI keys, Cosmos DB keys, SignalR connection strings, service principal secrets

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Pipeline observability — event processing latency, anomaly detection accuracy, alert delivery time, throughput metrics, dead-letter monitoring

optimization_tips: Use Stream Analytics for statistical anomaly detection (Z-score, IQR) as the first pass — only escalate genuinely anomalous events to Azure OpenAI for contextual interpretation, keeping AI costs proportional to actual anomalies (typically <2% of events)
Auto-scale Event Hubs throughput units based on ingestion patterns — most workloads have predictable daily cycles, so scale down during off-peak hours to reduce costs by 40%
Use Cosmos DB change feed instead of polling for downstream consumers — eliminates redundant reads and provides exactly-once processing semantics at no additional RU cost
Implement anomaly deduplication with a 5-minute suppression window — prevent alert storms from generating repeated Azure OpenAI calls for the same ongoing anomaly
Use Functions Consumption plan for dev/staging and reserve Premium only for production — the cold start penalty (1-2s) is acceptable for non-real-time environments
Configure Event Hubs capture to Blob Storage for historical analysis — cheaper than keeping high TU counts for replay, and enables batch analytics without impacting real-time pipeline
